2604.12461.title: CIA: Inferring the Communication Topology from LLM-based Multi-Agent Systems
2604.12461.paper_summary: LLM-based Multi-Agent Systems (MAS) have demonstrated remarkable capabilities in solving complex tasks. Central to MAS is the communication topology which governs how agents exchange information internally. Consequently, the security of communication topologies has attracted increasing attention. In this paper, we investigate a critical privacy risk: MAS communication topologies can be inferred under a restrictive black-box setting, exposing system vulnerabilities and posing significant intellectual property threats. To explore this risk, we propose Communication Inference Attack (CIA), a novel attack that constructs new adversarial queries to induce intermediate agents' reasoning outputs and models their semantic correlations through the proposed global bias disentanglement and LLM-guided weak supervision. Extensive experiments on MAS with optimized communication topologies demonstrate the effectiveness of CIA, achieving an average AUC of 0.87 and a peak AUC of up to 0.99, thereby revealing the substantial privacy risk in MAS.
2604.12461.paper_summary_zh: LLM 基於的多代理系統 (MAS) 在解決複雜任務方面展現了卓越的能力。MAS 的核心是通信拓撲，這決定了代理如何在內部交換信息。因此，通信拓撲的安全性引起了越來越多的關注。在本文中，我們調查了一個關鍵的隱私風險：MAS 通信拓撲可以在限制性的黑箱環境下被推斷出來，這暴露了系統的脆弱性並造成了重大的知識產權威脅。為了探討這一風險，我們提出了通信推斷攻擊 (CIA)，這是一種新穎的攻擊方式，通過構造新的對抗性查詢來誘導中間代理的推理輸出，並通過提出的全局偏差解耦和 LLM 指導的弱監督來建模其語義關聯。對優化通信拓撲的 MAS 進行的廣泛實驗證明了 CIA 的有效性，達到了 0.87 的平均 AUC 和高達 0.99 的峰值 AUC，從而揭示了 MAS 中存在的重大的隱私風險。
2604.12461.author: Yongxuan Wu et.al.
2604.12461.authors: Yongxuan Wu, Xixun Lin, He Zhang, Nan Sun, Kun Wang, Chuan Zhou, Shirui Pan, Yanan Cao
